description: NTS_LD Algorithm Input Example - Line Diffusion Method for 2D Neutron Transport

metadata.file_type: Plain text input (converted to JSON for reference)

program_overview.name: NTS_LD.c
program_overview.purpose: Solves 2D neutron transport equation using Line Diffusion (LD) iterative method
program_overview.key_difference_from_DD: Line Diffusion converges row-by-row (or column-by-column) instead of domain decomposition by blocks
program_overview.advantages: Better convergence for some problems, especially with strong anisotropy
program_overview.output_format: JSON with STATUS, ITER, CPU, MFLUX, MFLOW, XFLOW, YFLOW

example_1_simple_uniform_problem.name: Simple 2×2 Domain with Uniform Material
example_1_simple_uniform_problem.description: Simple domain 20×20 cm, one material zone, uniform sources, vacuum boundaries
example_1_simple_uniform_problem.physics: Tests basic convergence of LD method with simple geometry

expected_output.expected_pattern: Symmetric flux due to uniform problem
expected_output.convergence_speed: Faster than DD for this simple case

example_2_heterogeneous_two_materials.name: Heterogeneous with Two Materials
example_2_heterogeneous_two_materials.description: Checkerboard pattern with Material 1 (low absorption) and Material 2 (high absorption)
example_2_heterogeneous_two_materials.physics: Tests LD behavior with material discontinuities

json_format.geometry: Checkerboard pattern creates strong material interfaces
json_format.note: LD method may show different convergence behavior than DD due to material heterogeneity

example_3_anisotropic_sources.name: Problem with Anisotropic External Sources
example_3_anisotropic_sources.description: Variable sources with directional bias to test LD anisotropy handling
example_3_anisotropic_sources.physics: LD excels when sources have strong directional preferences

json_format.spatial: 3 regions in X and Y (non-uniform spacing)
json_format.note: LD method handles asymmetric problems efficiently

example_4_fine_mesh.name: Fine Mesh Problem
example_4_fine_mesh.description: Finer spatial discretization for higher accuracy

json_format.note: Stricter tolerance and finer mesh increase computation time

convergence.LD: Typically faster for problems with weak anisotropy
convergence.DD: More stable for strongly anisotropic problems

memory.DD: Higher memory due to block decomposition

applicability.LD: Better for 1D/2D geometries with line-like structures
applicability.DD: Better for 2D problems with 2D decomposition benefits

input_parameters.note: Both use same input format (11 parameters)

ZMAP.name: Zone Map
ZMAP.format: NR_Y × NR_X matrix with zone indices (1 to NZ)

QMAP.name: Source Map
QMAP.format: NR_Y × NR_X matrix with source values (≥ 0)
